office_does: One of two House members for LD 46 (northeast Seattle); writes and votes on state law and the state operating, capital, and transportation budgets.
race_blurb: Both candidates advance to the general regardless of the primary result. A Democratic incumbent with passed bills and committee leadership faces a first-time independent whose core message is rejecting party-line politics.

candidates.summary: Incumbent state representative and disability-equity specialist with a legislative record on food access, caregiver workforce, and disability policy.
highlights: Passed HB 2294 banning covenants that block new grocery stores and pharmacies, and HB 1395 removing caregiver employment barriers [S3][S4]
First Middle Eastern woman elected to the Washington Legislature; Vice Chair of Civil Rights & Judiciary [S1][S2]
Endorsed by the 46th District Democrats, Pro-Choice Washington, WA State Labor Council, AG Nick Brown, and Rep. Pramila Jayapal [S1]

experience.basis: Incumbent with committee leadership roles and multiple bills passed, facing a first-time opponent.

social.basis: Endorsed by Pro-Choice Washington; civil-rights committee leadership and equity-focused career.

safety.basis: Board member of the Washington Coalition for Police Accountability; no direct policy statements in the dossier.

candidates.summary: First-time candidate preferring the 'Independent Party' whose central message is constituent responsiveness over party-line politics.
highlights: 35 years with the Lake Washington Education Association; long record in sports officiating and water-ski organizations [S1]
Supports 'Washington State citizenship for undocumented residents' and a three-year college path [S1]
Runs as an independent because 'Olympia needs a culture change'; calls for fiscal accountability before spending [S1][S2]

experience.basis: First-time candidate with no elected experience, running against a sitting legislator.

reform.basis: Runs as an independent against party-line politics, arguing officials follow party leadership rather than constituents.

immigration.basis: Supports 'Washington State citizenship for undocumented residents.'

spending.basis: Calls for fiscal accountability and 'a government that listens to citizens before it spends their money.'
